text:
of a competent person to provide a non-skilled service, regardless of the importance of 
the service to the patient, does not make it a skilled service when a nurse provides the 
service. 

A service that, by its nature, requires the skills of a nurse to be provided safely and 
effectively continues to be a skilled service even if it is taught to the patient, the patient's 
family, or other caregivers. 

The skilled nursing service must be reasonable and necessary to the diagnosis and
